title: canthium ripae
intro: Canthium ripae, also known as the forest tea plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in various forms for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Canthium ripae include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other gynecological problems. The plant is often prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in the form of a decoction or tincture for its medicinal properties.
